promotion_candidates.decision_decision: Do not promote the dInput-only Torch row. The full Torch native backward with dweight/dbias is slower than CUDA, the full Triton atomic prototype is also slower plus has an FP32 gradient-buffer contract mismatch, and the focused Torch dInput-plus-BF16-grads hybrid is much slower than the CUDA full-backward baseline.
decision_evidence: 2026-05-20 target-context refresh: dInput-only Torch native 217.568 us; Triton dInput-only 228.320 us
2026-05-20 target-context refresh: full Torch native backward 416.224 us; Triton atomic FP32-grad backward 363.680 us; CUDA full backward remains about 290 us
2026-05-21 focused target-context hybrid: Torch BackwardDInputNativePlusGrads 1964.480 us with BF16 dweight/dbias
promotion_candidates.decision_note: Torch native dInput-only output-mask row is useful for backward decomposition work but needs dweight/dbias before trainer promotion.
promotion_candidates.promotion_gate: add dweight/dbias accumulation before considering trainer promotion

promotion_candidates.decision_decision: Keep Torch native LayerNorm forward as reference/operator evidence only. It does not produce the saved mean/rstd state required by the GPT-2 trainer backward contract; stats-compatible Torch compositions are much slower than CUDA.
decision_evidence: 2026-05-22 optional refresh scratch/sm120_rounds/codex_sm120_current_optional_refresh_20260522: Torch native LayerNorm forward C=768 measured 139.712 us versus CUDA 140.567 us, a 0.608% operator-only edge.
The trainer LayerNorm forward contract stores mean and rstd for backward; the Torch native forward row has no saved mean/rstd.
2026-05-22 optional refresh stats-compatible Torch row Torch LayerNorm ForwardWithStats C=768 measured 2183.296 us, far slower than the CUDA trainer row.
promotion_candidates.decision_note: Native Torch is useful where saved mean/rstd are not needed; trainer LayerNorm still needs stats-compatible state.
promotion_candidates.promotion_gate: refresh same-session baseline, produce trainer-compatible saved state, then rerun parity and TinyStories

selected_backend_rows.decision_decision: Do not broaden the SM120 direct-cuBLAS dInput selector to attention projection. selection_policy: Fastest observed row per exact suite/kernel/shape. Rows without an existing trainer call path remain reference/operator prototypes until route-specific correctness and TinyStories smoke evidence proves promotion. Repo-local decisions mark refreshed rejects and structurally non-promotable rows inactive without deleting their evidence.
